jokes.text: We now go live to our eye-in-the-sky hovercopter on the scene of that terrible hovercopter crash. Jim? The news is not good. I've just learned that my final words were 'back to you, Linda.'
jokes.setup: News reporter goes live to helicopter covering a crash
jokes.punchline: The reporter realizes he's reporting on his own death in real-time
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that subverts news reporting conventions. The matter-of-fact delivery of impossible situation creates perfect absurd logic.

jokes.text: One for the blooper reel.
jokes.setup: Reporter just announced his own death
jokes.punchline: Casual dismissal as just another workplace mishap
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that maintains the absurd tone with incredible economy. The understatement makes the dark humor even funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, the news is so violent. Let's watch Rachael Ray instead. Oh, no, wait, there might be chopping.
jokes.setup: Character wants to avoid violent news
jokes.punchline: Even cooking shows are too violent for her extreme squeamishness
jokes.explanation: Good character moment that establishes Amy's squeamishness through escalating absurdity.

jokes.text: God, what a wuss. Stop being such a spineless jellyfish. You know full well I am more closely related to the sea cucumber.
jokes.setup: Amy calls Kif a spineless jellyfish
jokes.punchline: Kif's pedantic correction about his actual species
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif moment - his pedantic nature makes him correct an insult with biological accuracy. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Not where it counts. (Whimpers)
jokes.setup: Kif's scientific correction
jokes.punchline: Amy's devastating comeback that emasculates him completely
jokes.explanation: Brutal comeback with perfect economy. Kif's whimper is the cherry on top.

jokes.text: That is one sexy bridge abutment.
jokes.setup: Bender looking at his graffiti artwork on a bridge
jokes.punchline: Bender being sexually attracted to his own vandalism
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender narcissism combined with his tendency to sexualize inappropriate things.

jokes.text: Free corn? Well, that'll suit me just fine. (Engine revs, tires screech) (Hyperchicken screams, car crashes)
jokes.setup: Hyperchicken sees free corn sign
jokes.punchline: Immediate car crash from distracted driving
jokes.explanation: Simple slapstick gag. The chicken's enthusiasm for corn leading to immediate disaster works but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Well, well. Time to beat him his rights.
jokes.setup: Police officer arresting Bender
jokes.punchline: Corruption of 'read him his rights' into literal violence
jokes.explanation: Solid wordplay that subverts police procedure expectations. Good economy but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: You know, that don't look half bad on your buttocks. It does sort of class up the place.
jokes.setup: Bender getting graffiti tattooed on him as punishment
jokes.punchline: Police officer admitting the vandalism actually improves the jail
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic twist where the punishment becomes an improvement. Solid but not exceptional.

jokes.text: (Clock chimes) Booming voice: The time is 4:00 A.M.!
jokes.setup: Normal clock chiming
jokes.punchline: Unnecessarily dramatic announcement of the obvious
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist gag. The over-the-top announcement of mundane information is mildly amusing.

jokes.text: I'm not just some piece of tofu, Amy.
jokes.setup: Kif defending himself in relationship argument
jokes.punchline: Using tofu as metaphor for being bland and squishy
jokes.explanation: Perfect metaphor for Kif's character - he compares himself to the blandest food possible while trying to assert masculinity.

jokes.text: It's been ages, according to your wildly inappropriate hunk-of-the-month calendar.
jokes.setup: Kif complaining about their relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Amy has a sexy calendar that's making their problems worse
jokes.explanation: Good character detail that adds to both the relationship conflict and Amy's personality. Visual element enhances the joke.

jokes.text: Leave Todd out of this. Thanks, babe. Also, today is Canadian Independence Day. (Plays wolf-whistle)
jokes.setup: Amy defending her calendar
jokes.punchline: The calendar responds and gives irrelevant information with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Escalates the absurdity by making the calendar interactive. The random Canadian fact and wolf whistle add layers of weirdness.

jokes.text: Oop, that's Bender's ringtone. I recognize the smell.
jokes.setup: Phone ringing with burping sound
jokes.punchline: Amy identifies Bender by the smell of his ringtone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd logic that Bender's burping ringtone has a smell. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: You know that floor safe where you keep ten grand? There's five grand in there.
jokes.setup: Bender asking Amy for bail money
jokes.punchline: Casually revealing he's already stolen half her money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment - his casual criminality and the matter-of-fact delivery make this a standout character joke.

jokes.text: Hey, Bender, you ever kill a man with a sock? It ain't so hard.
jokes.setup: Menacing prison inmate talking to Bender
jokes.punchline: Casually discussing murder with a household item
jokes.explanation: Standard dark prison humor. The mundane weapon choice adds some originality but it's fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Tell me, have things changed on the outside? Is food finally in pill form? What about pills? Are they in food form?
jokes.setup: Bender asking about changes while he was in prison
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about food and pills switching forms
jokes.explanation: Smart reversal joke with good escalation. The circular logic creates a memorable moment with strong quotability.

jokes.text: Hey, hot stuff, I'm Larry the murder burglar.
jokes.setup: Criminal introducing himself
jokes.punchline: Combining two crimes into one professional title
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay that treats crime specialization like a professional credential. Good economy but not groundbreaking.

jokes.text: I just don't get why you have to flirt with every bad boy in sight.
jokes.setup: Kif complaining about Amy's behavior
jokes.punchline: Sets up the immediate demonstration of his point
jokes.explanation: Functional setup line that establishes the pattern about to be demonstrated. Works as part of larger sequence.

jokes.text: Nobody move or sweet cheeks here gets it. (Giggles) Ooh, you're bad.
jokes.setup: Armed robber threatening Amy
jokes.punchline: Amy flirts with him instead of being scared
jokes.explanation: Perfect demonstration of Amy's bad boy attraction at the worst possible moment. Great character consistency and timing.

jokes.text: Mama said Spock you out.
jokes.setup: Robber trying to sound threatening
jokes.punchline: Garbled combination of 'knock you out' and Star Trek reference
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't quite land. The malapropism feels forced rather than character-driven.

jokes.text: Pardon my language, but I have had it with you ruffling my petticoats.
jokes.setup: Kif getting angry at Amy
jokes.punchline: Apologizing for mild language while using flowery Victorian metaphors
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kif character moment. His politeness even when angry and the feminine metaphor create a memorable line.

jokes.text: It's called 'Let's go already.'
jokes.setup: Fry offering to play a song to cheer Amy up
jokes.punchline: Song title that expresses his impatience with the situation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor where the song title breaks the fourth wall. Shows Fry's impatience with typical sitcom comfort moments.

jokes.text: Yeah, the game of old maid.
jokes.setup: Amy saying she's back in the game
jokes.punchline: Bender's cruel but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that's perfectly mean in character for Bender. Excellent economy and timing.

jokes.text: You're damn right, and before you ask, this is mine, too. Where am I? Is this TBS?
jokes.setup: Agent claiming ownership of something
jokes.punchline: Complete confusion about basic reality and network identity
jokes.explanation: Mildly amusing agent stereotype confusion. The network reference adds some meta humor but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: No, technically we were fon-fon-rus, so we weren't really married.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if Amy and Kif are getting divorced
jokes.punchline: Amy reveals they had an alien relationship status that's not quite marriage
jokes.explanation: Good worldbuilding humor that uses established alien relationship terminology. The made-up word 'fon-fon-rus' sounds appropriately alien and silly.

jokes.text: Why did Kif dump you like a sack of yesterday's turds?
jokes.setup: Bender asking about Amy's breakup delicately
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude metaphor delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender insensitivity. The contrast between 'delicate' setup and crude delivery works well.

jokes.text: Shut up, baby, you love it.
jokes.setup: Amy asking why Bender is mean to her
jokes.punchline: Bender's signature dismissive catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender catchphrase that perfectly encapsulates his character. Highly quotable and establishes the romantic tension.

jokes.text: Remember, love between a human and a robot is taboo.
jokes.setup: Amy establishing the forbidden nature of their relationship
jokes.punchline: Sets up the glass tube contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good setup that establishes stakes while setting up the visual contradiction. Functions well as part of sequence.

jokes.text: I think I'm coming down with circus-itis. (Sneezes, clown horn honks) I thought circus-itis only affected children. Children of all ages.
jokes.setup: Fry claiming to have a made-up disease
jokes.punchline: Sound effect confirms it, Amy's response references circus announcements
jokes.explanation: Clever disease concept with good sound effect payoff. The 'children of all ages' reference elevates it.

jokes.text: I thought circus-itis only affected children. Children of all ages.
jokes.setup: Someone noting circus-itis affects children
jokes.punchline: Professor clarifies 'children of all ages' - the circus catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that completes the circus reference with the classic announcement phrase. Extends the original joke logically.

jokes.text: Uh... blood. Uh, you know... Shaving of the face beard.
jokes.setup: Professor noticing something on Bender's face
jokes.punchline: Bender's terrible improvised excuse with awkward phrasing
jokes.explanation: Good awkward lying moment. The redundant phrasing 'face beard' adds to Bender's obvious panic.

jokes.text: I stole 'em from her. Yeah, that's it, call the cops.
jokes.setup: Bender caught wearing Amy's sweatpants
jokes.punchline: Admitting to theft as the better excuse, then challenging them sarcastically
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - theft is preferable to admitting romance. The sarcastic challenge shows his confidence.

jokes.text: Amy, I reckon sweet talk don't come natural to me, but of everyone I've ever dated, you are probably in the top ten.
jokes.setup: Bender attempting romantic talk
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that places Amy barely in top ten
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment that shows Bender trying to be romantic but failing spectacularly. Highly quotable and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Aw, you always say just the wrong thing in just the right way.
jokes.setup: Bender's terrible romantic attempt
jokes.punchline: Amy finding it charming despite its awfulness
jokes.explanation: Perfect response that shows Amy's attraction to Bender's unique brand of awfulness. Great character insight.

jokes.text: The good book sayeth... a robot shall not lie down with a human, nor do it standing up, nor at any angle in between.
jokes.setup: Preacherbot condemning robosexuality
jokes.punchline: Exhaustively covering all possible sexual positions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that parodies biblical language while being absurdly specific about sexual positions.

jokes.text: Look, a single mother! Let's get her!
jokes.setup: Angry mob worked up about robosexuality
jokes.punchline: Immediately distracted by different moral target
jokes.explanation: Sharp social satire about moral panic mobs finding new targets. Good economy and misdirection.

jokes.text: I've got sunny, snow flurries, hail with onions, even my grandmother's cranberry-raisin typhoon, but no tornado.
jokes.setup: Professor listing his pickled weather collection
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd weather/food combinations culminating in family recipe
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist concept that escalates well. The grandmother's recipe adds personal touch to the weirdness.

jokes.text: Is everybody in position?! What?! Is someone talking?! Okay, good! On three! One! What did she say?! Two! Wait, I'm not in position! Three! I'm ready for the countdown! I'm not hearing anyone! Abort mission! She said go!
jokes.setup: Professor coordinating tornado capture
jokes.punchline: Complete communication breakdown in high winds
jokes.explanation: Good slapstick sequence showing complete coordination failure. The overlapping confusion builds well.

jokes.text: They're missing out on some hot pickle action.
jokes.setup: Professor noting Amy and Bender are missing
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual description of his pickle-making process
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre that works because the Professor is oblivious to the sexual meaning while Amy and Bender are actually having sex nearby.

jokes.text: Sounds like a muskrat's caught in there.
jokes.setup: Hearing Amy and Bender in the tornado container
jokes.punchline: Innocent misinterpretation of sexual sounds
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that maintains the secret while creating awkward humor. Hermes's practical interpretation works.

jokes.text: Sweet tornado of Barbado. Amy and Bender have been torn to shreds. Oh, the humanity. Also Bender.
jokes.setup: Professor seeing torn clothing
jokes.punchline: Dramatic mourning followed by afterthought inclusion of Bender
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Hindenburg disaster with nice afterthought tag for Bender. The escalation works well.

jokes.text: Oh, the humanity. Also Bender.
jokes.setup: Lamenting the apparent deaths of Amy and Bender
jokes.punchline: References famous Hindenburg quote but adds Bender as an afterthought
jokes.explanation: Nice reference joke that treats Bender as separate from humanity. The casual addition 'Also Bender' perfectly captures his second-class status.

jokes.text: What about on the roof? What about in the gutter?
jokes.setup: Professor saying not under his roof
jokes.punchline: Bender literalistically exploring alternative locations
jokes.explanation: Good literal interpretation joke that shows Bender's focus on finding loopholes. Escalates appropriately.

jokes.text: What about on the roof? Get your mind out of the gutter. What about in the gutter?
jokes.setup: Professor says not under his roof, Amy suggests alternatives
jokes.punchline: Amy takes 'gutter' literally when Professor uses it figuratively
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Amy's literal interpretation of figurative language creating more inappropriate suggestions.

jokes.text: Or one.
jokes.setup: Leela talking about two consenting adults
jokes.punchline: Bender's casual admission of robotic masturbation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant economy and perfect character moment. The casual delivery of inappropriate information is peak Bender.

jokes.text: Wong ranch no brokebacking
jokes.setup: Amy's parents' reaction to her relationship
jokes.punchline: Ranch sign referencing Brokeback Mountain
jokes.explanation: Simple visual reference that works for the situation. Not particularly clever but serves its purpose.

jokes.text: Look, we're your family, and if you can't talk about your problem with us, that would be great.
jokes.setup: Mom trying to be supportive
jokes.punchline: Subverts expectation by preferring not to discuss it
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that subverts typical family support dialogue. Great character insight and quotability.

jokes.text: You finish that word, you kill your parents!
jokes.setup: Amy about to say 'robosexual'
jokes.punchline: Father's extreme reaction to just hearing the word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing parental horror. The extreme reaction to mere words works for the character.

jokes.text: Stop seducing him, you hussy! I'm attracted to Bender, not this emotionless wine bucket! Hopes... deleted.
jokes.setup: Mom accusing Amy of seducing wine robot
jokes.punchline: Robot's crushing disappointment expressed in computer terms
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with perfect robot dialogue. The 'hopes deleted' is both funny and touching.

jokes.text: Can I get a amen? I'll take-a three men. Holla!
jokes.setup: Preacherbot asking for congregation response
jokes.punchline: Misinterprets as ordering men, adds modern slang
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay that doesn't quite land. The 'holla' addition feels forced rather than natural.

jokes.text: I want to be a grandmother... nine months from five minutes from now.
jokes.setup: Mom wanting Amy to pick a human male
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific timeline showing her desperation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that shows the mother's desperation through specific timing. The math makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Look at all that awkward winking? It must be love!
jokes.setup: Amy and Fry's terrible acting
jokes.punchline: Mom misreading obvious deception as romance
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy that plays on parental wishful thinking. The 'awkward winking' description adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Look at the rack on that one! I mean... That one on the rack!
jokes.setup: Robot looking at human female torture dummy
jokes.punchline: Catches himself making sexual comment, awkwardly tries to cover
jokes.explanation: Standard double entendre with predictable cover-up. The torture device context adds some originality but not much.

jokes.text: Look at the rack on that one! I mean... That one on the rack!
jokes.setup: Robot trying to resist attraction to human female demons
jokes.punchline: Freudian slip about breasts, quickly covers with torture equipment reference
jokes.explanation: Standard Freudian slip comedy that works in context of conversion therapy parody. The quick correction attempt makes it more believable.

jokes.text: Now, work it hard! Harder! Till the sin explodes outcha!
jokes.setup: Preacherbot directing therapy session to wrestle with demons
jokes.punchline: Unintentionally sexual coaching language during 'conversion' therapy
jokes.explanation: Good satire of conversion therapy that highlights its absurdity through unintentional sexual language. The irony is obvious but effective.

jokes.text: Mmm... mercy.
jokes.setup: Watching robots 'wrestle' with human-shaped demons
jokes.punchline: Preacherbot getting inappropriately aroused by his own therapy
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing the preacher's hypocrisy. Excellent economy with just two words.

jokes.text: I love Amy, and I'm tired of pussyfootcupping around. I finally found someone I want to spend the rest of her life with.
jokes.setup: Bender making romantic declaration
jokes.punchline: Malapropism combining pussyfooting around and other phrase, plus possessive error
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender malapropism that shows his attempt at eloquence backfiring. The possessive error adds another layer.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm just glad I didn't live to see this day. Hey, wait a second. (Faint heartbeat) Noooo...!
jokes.setup: Professor's dramatic death declaration
jokes.punchline: Realizes he's still alive and is horrified by this fact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal that subverts the typical 'glad I didn't live to see this' trope. The horror at being alive is perfect dark comedy.

jokes.text: You mean Space-Tax-Achusetts?
jokes.setup: Mention of Space Massachusetts
jokes.punchline: Dad joke about Massachusetts taxes
jokes.explanation: Predictable dad joke about Massachusetts taxes. Functional but not particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: (Jamaican accent): Yah, mon! You got to legalize it! We're talking about robosexual marriage. We're talking about lots of stuff.
jokes.setup: Jamaican man supporting legalization
jokes.punchline: He's talking about marijuana, not marriage
jokes.explanation: Standard misdirection joke about marijuana legalization. The follow-up line helps clarify but doesn't add much.

jokes.text: Cool. Can you turn into a race car? Nah, I'm just a pre-op transformer.
jokes.setup: Human asking if robot can transform
jokes.punchline: Robot using transgender terminology for transformation ability
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that connects Transformers with gender transition terminology. Good economy and structure.

jokes.text: Our poodle has two daddies!
jokes.setup: Listing various accepted marriage types
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of gay pet ownership
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about modern family structures. Works as part of the list but isn't particularly standout.

jokes.text: even ghost and horse
jokes.setup: Listing acceptable marriage combinations
jokes.punchline: Completely absurd pairing that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to complete absurdity. The combination is so random it becomes funny through pure weirdness.

jokes.text: As a mother, those things worry me.
jokes.setup: Following the admission that they couldn't think of horrible consequences
jokes.punchline: Mother claims to be worried about the non-existent things they just admitted they couldn't think of
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up that extends the absurdist political ad satire. The circular logic of being worried about imaginary concerns is beautifully executed.

jokes.text: We can't compete against that much stock footage of clouds!
jokes.setup: Watching anti-Prop Infinity commercial
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall to comment on cheap production techniques
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor that acknowledges cheap commercial production. Self-referential without being too obvious.

jokes.text: I'm our A in the hole!
jokes.setup: Bender claiming to be their ace in the hole
jokes.punchline: Malapropism that creates unintended sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Decent malapropism that creates accidental innuendo. Works for Bender's character but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: After all, our love isn't any different from yours... except it's hotter, 'cause I'm involved.
jokes.setup: Bender making equality argument
jokes.punchline: Undermines his point with pure narcissism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment that subverts his own equality message with narcissism. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: I haven't heard such an eloquent speech since Bill Shatner explained why he couldn't pay me back.
jokes.setup: Takei praising Bender's speech
jokes.punchline: Reference to personal financial dispute with Shatner
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference that adds personal detail. Works because it's specific enough to feel real.

jokes.text: You wouldn't know perversion if it put clamps on your testicles!
jokes.setup: Bender defending against perversion accusations
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and inappropriate analogy
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Bender comeback. The specific imagery makes it memorable and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: But then, 43 years later, she left me... for a robot! How could she do that to me, her Farnypoo!
jokes.setup: Professor's tragic love story
jokes.punchline: Lost girlfriend to robot, plus embarrassing pet name
jokes.explanation: Good emotional setup with the pet name 'Farnypoo' adding both humor and pathos. Explains Professor's prejudice.

jokes.text: She didn't just leave me for a robot, she was a robot! Oh, God, her name wasn't Eunice, it was Unit! Unit 47!
jokes.setup: Professor's revelation about his lost love
jokes.punchline: Double twist - she was a robot with designation instead of name
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double reversal that completely reframes the Professor's motivation. The progression from Eunice to Unit 47 is perfect.

jokes.text: What's next, gay robosexual marriage?
jokes.setup: Robosexual marriage has been legalized
jokes.punchline: Suggesting an even more specific subcategory
jokes.explanation: Good escalation that parodies slippery slope arguments. The specificity adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Monogamous?
jokes.setup: Amy talking about legal monogamous marriage
jokes.punchline: Bender's horror at the concept of monogamy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender moment with incredible economy. One word that perfectly captures his character and sets up the ending.
